articles.title: Karney Snags $34M Industrial Refi in Southern California
articles.narrative: Karney Properties, a prominent industrial operator, has secured $33.5 million in fixed-rate refinancing from Nationwide for a 178,454-square-foot Class A industrial facility in Cerritos, CA. The property, located at 15959 Piuma Avenue, is a single-tenant building adjacent to Interstate 605, featuring 32-foot clear heights and excess trailer storage. The financing was arranged by JLL Capital Markets.

data_points.notable_features: Adjacent to Interstate 605, features 32-foot clear heights, dock-high loading doors, and excess trailer storage.

articles.market_intelligence: Greater Los Angeles industrial market saw 13 move-ins versus five move-outs for spaces 100,000 square feet or more in Q1. The broader market experienced negative 765,919 square feet of year-to-date net absorption, with total vacancy at 7.3 percent and average asking rent at $1.38 per square foot per month.

articles.title: Targo Capital Partners Buys 185 East Houston Street, Part of $81M Portfolio
articles.narrative: Targo Capital Partners has acquired 185 East Houston Street, a six-story, 31-unit apartment building on Manhattan’s Lower East Side, for $30.8 million. This transaction is part of a larger $81 million portfolio acquisition by Targo Capital Partners, comprising seven prewar residential properties from S&H Equities. The portfolio includes 125 residential units and ground-floor retail space across various Lower Manhattan neighborhoods.

data_points.address: 185 East Houston Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Prewar residential property with ground-floor retail.
data_points.project_notes: Part of a larger $81 million portfolio acquisition of seven properties totaling 125 residential units. The other properties are located at 90 Rivington Street, 96 Orchard Street, 156 Orchard Street, 126 First Avenue, 153 Avenue C and 38 Carmine Street.

articles.title: New York’s Pied-à-Terre Tax Is Bad Policy. But It Shouldn’t Stop Development Land Sales.
articles.narrative: The author argues that New York's new pied-à-terre tax, while disruptive to the luxury condominium and cooperative market in the short term due to uncertainty, should not significantly impact the value of development land being acquired today. This is because current land buyers are underwriting projects that will be completed years from now, by which time the tax's initial phase will have ended, and legal challenges or amendments are likely. The author suggests that existing condominium inventory will face near-term headwinds, but development land values are more influenced by future market conditions than current ones. However, a key caveat is that if the state legislature extends the current high tax rates beyond 2028, the equation for development land values would change dramatically.

articles.title: New York State Lawmakers Pass One-Year Data Center Construction Moratorium
articles.narrative: New York State lawmakers have passed a one-year moratorium on the construction of new large data centers, aiming to establish regulations for the rapidly expanding industry. The legislation, which passed both the Senate and Assembly on the final day of the legislative session, seeks to address concerns regarding environmental impact, infrastructure capacity, and the protection of local communities. While Governor Kathy Hochul's office has not indicated whether she will sign the bill, the administration has previously emphasized the importance of ensuring that data centers cover the costs of their utility expansion to prevent rising expenses for consumers, especially with the increasing demand driven by artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Olmstead Buys 19 West 44th Street From Savanna at a 45% Haircut
articles.narrative: Olmstead Properties, in partnership with its investment platform Vertex, has acquired the 18-story office building at 19 West 44th Street in Midtown Manhattan for $108 million. The seller, Savanna, incurred a significant loss on the transaction, having purchased the property for $195 million in 2017. The new ownership plans amenity upgrades and a prebuilt suite program to attract new tenants, building on recent capital improvements and existing strong tenancy.

data_points.address: 19 West 44th Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located between Fifth and Sixth avenues, the property recently underwent $30 million in capital improvements.
data_points.project_notes: Savanna acquired the property in October 2017 for $195 million from Deka Immobilien GmbH. The acquisition financing will also support planned amenity upgrades and a prebuilt suite program.

articles.title: Rosewood Property Co. Begins Leasing 338-Unit Apartment Community in Plano
articles.narrative: Rosewood Property Co. has commenced leasing at The Buckley, a 338-unit apartment community in Plano, TX. This development is a sister property to The Ludlow, both situated within the expansive Heritage Creekside master-planned community. The project offers a variety of unit types and a comprehensive suite of amenities, with construction financing provided by Broadway Bank and equity investment from MetLife Investment Management.

data_points.notable_features: Amenities include a pool, multiple lounge areas, a club and card room, fitness center, coworking areas, and a dog park.
data_points.project_notes: Located within the 156-acre Heritage Creekside master-planned development. Sister property to The Ludlow.

articles.title: Arvato USA Signs 269,600 SF Industrial Lease in Denton, Texas
articles.narrative: Arvato USA, a Germany-based third-party logistics provider, has signed a 269,600-square-foot industrial lease for the entirety of Denton Logistics Center in Denton, Texas. The newly constructed building offers 32-foot clear heights, providing modern specifications for logistics operations.

data_points.notable_features: Newly constructed building with 32-foot clear heights.

articles.title: Berkadia Brokers Sale of 186-Unit Seniors Housing Property Near Houston
articles.narrative: Town Land and Arcole Partners have acquired Grand Living at Tuscan Lakes, a 186-unit seniors housing property in League City, Texas, from Bow River Capital and Ryan Cos. The recently opened community, completed in 2023, offers a full spectrum of care options including independent living, assisted living, and memory care, complemented by a robust amenity package.

data_points.address: 1850 E League City Pkwy, League City, TX
data_points.notable_features: Offers independent living, assisted living, and memory care residences with amenities including a pool, theater, spa, fitness center, library, and lounge.

articles.title: Creative Action to Open 25,000 SF Headquarters in Austin’s Mueller District
articles.narrative: Creative Action, a youth arts education organization, is set to open a new 25,000-square-foot headquarters in Austin's Mueller district. The facility will be split across two levels, dedicating 15,000 square feet to performing arts and communal gatherings, with the remaining 10,000 square feet allocated for administrative and programming functions. This move signifies a significant expansion for the organization within a prominent mixed-use redevelopment.

data_points.notable_features: Located within Mueller, a 700-acre mixed-use district redeveloped from a former municipal airport.

articles.title: Panepinto Properties Begins Leasing 53-Story Apartment Tower in Jersey City
articles.narrative: Panepinto Properties has commenced leasing at 505 Summit, a 53-story apartment tower in Jersey City's Journal Square. The development offers 605 residential units along with street-level retail space and a range of amenities. Construction on the project, financed by Pacific Life, began in the third quarter of 2023 and topped out last summer.

data_points.notable_features: 53-story apartment tower with studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom floor plans, a landscaped public plaza, parking garage, and 304 bicycle storage spaces. Residential amenities include an outdoor terrace with a pool, grilling stations, a resident lounge, coworking spaces, sports simulator, and a fitness center.
data_points.project_notes: Construction began in Q3 2023 and topped out in summer 2025.

articles.title: Entre Commercial Realty closes 55,488-square-foot industrial lease in Lake Zurich
articles.narrative: Entre Commercial Realty facilitated a 55,488-square-foot industrial lease at 1290 Ensell Road in Lake Zurich, Illinois. The tenant, represented by Entre Commercial, sought a larger facility to accommodate its expansion requirements. The property's features, including clear height ceilings and loading docks, made it a suitable choice for the tenant's operational needs.

data_points.notable_features: Features 25-foot clear height ceilings, 8,248 square feet of office space, four interior loading docks, and three oversized drive-in doors.

articles.title: St. Louis’ Lawrence Group adds controller
articles.narrative: The Lawrence Group, a St. Louis-based firm, has appointed Katlyn Garcia as its new Controller. Garcia returns to the company after a previous tenure in its accounting department, bringing recent experience as a Controller for Rick's Ace Hardware and accounting management roles at other St. Louis-area firms. In her new capacity, she will oversee the firm's accounting operations, financial reporting, compliance, and financial management strategy.

articles.title: Marcus & Millichap closes sale of eight-unit apartment property in Chicago’s Old Town neighborhood
articles.narrative: Marcus & Millichap has brokered the sale of an eight-unit multifamily property in Chicago's Old Town neighborhood for $2.5 million. The transaction involved 1503 North Weiland Street (Chicago) LLC as the seller and Sedgwick Properties LLC as the buyer. The property's prime location near active retail and dining corridors was a key factor in the sale.

data_points.notable_features: Features five one-bedroom units and three two-bedroom units, located steps from Wells Street and North Avenue with immediate access to retail and dining.

articles.title: Venture One Real Estate acquires 59,611-square-foot industrial building in Mundelein
articles.narrative: Venture One Real Estate, through its fund VK Industrial VII, LP, has acquired a 59,611-square-foot multi-tenant industrial building in Mundelein, Illinois. The property was fully leased at the time of closing, indicating strong market demand for industrial space in the area. This acquisition expands Venture One's industrial portfolio with a well-occupied asset.

data_points.notable_features: Multi-tenant building demised into three units, featuring four docks and two drive-in doors.

articles.title: Skender tops out at new Westfield police headquarters
articles.narrative: Skender, serving as general contractor, has topped out construction on the new Westfield Police Department headquarters, a $35 million facility developed in partnership with Garmong. The 50,000-square-foot building, located on an 11.7-acre site along Grand Park Boulevard in Westfield, Indiana, is designed to replace the city's outdated public safety building and support future growth. The project, which began in late 2025, is anticipated to be completed in 2027 and will consolidate police and informatics departments, enhancing public safety and technology functions.

data_points.notable_features: Designed for future expansion up to 80,000 square feet, including a planned 30,000-square-foot addition over 15-20 years; consolidates police and informatics departments; features collaborative workspaces, wellness areas, an emergency operations center, and community training space.
data_points.project_notes: Public-private partnership; replaces existing Westfield Public Safety Building at State Road 32 and Dartown Road.

articles.title: Housing Notes: Amazonification of summer rentals
articles.narrative: The East End summer rental market is experiencing a significant shift from early, predictable leasing to a last-minute, "just-in-time" model, driven by a generational change in consumer behavior. Renters are increasingly treating the process like ordering from Amazon, waiting until closer to key holidays like Memorial Day and July 4th to book properties. This pattern, observed by local real estate experts, suggests a behavioral evolution since the pandemic rather than a weakening market, though it introduces greater anxiety for landlords. The trend is also noted in other high-priced, second-home markets across the U.S., where segments less dependent on rising mortgage rates are seeing more rental activity.

articles.title: The Data Drop: In a pricey world, NYC’s builders pivot to redevelopments
articles.narrative: An analysis of New York City permit data reveals a significant shift in development trends, with builders increasingly prioritizing redevelopment projects over new ground-up construction due to persistently high financing costs. This trend is particularly evident in Manhattan, where office-to-residential conversions are prevalent, and in Brooklyn, where expansions of single and two-family homes are becoming common. This pivot allows developers to maintain a steady construction pipeline despite rising interest rates and construction expenses.
articles.market_intelligence: High mortgage rates are making it increasingly difficult for homeowners to give up their low-interest-rate loans, contributing to lower homeowner mobility in Sun Belt markets. The Dallas-Fort Worth housing market is normalizing post-pandemic, with increased sales volume but a dip in average prices for top brokers. A five-year rent freeze in New York City is projected to have a limited impact on multifamily loan defaults, affecting only 6-8% of loans according to Moody's.

articles.title: The real estate bills Albany passed, killed or kicked down the road
articles.narrative: The New York State Legislature concluded its session with several real estate-related bills passed, while others failed to advance. Key legislation approved includes a bill requiring seller or landlord consent for private listings, a one-year moratorium on new data centers due to energy and water concerns, and increased experience requirements for real estate broker licenses. Notably, a highly anticipated bill that would have eased the process for upstate communities to adopt rent stabilization failed to pass, with its sponsor citing limited time for consensus-building after protracted budget negotiations.

people.name: Brian Kavanagh
people.title: State Senator and housing committee chair

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Real Estate Board of New York

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: New York State Building and Construction Trades Council

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: New York State Association of Realtors

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Homeowners for an Affordable New York

articles.title: David and Michael Shabsels’ summer camp empire files for bankruptcy
articles.narrative: Simad Holdings, an American summer camp operator, and its affiliates have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in New Jersey, listing liabilities between $500 million and $1 billion. The filing comes after owners David and Michael Shabsels missed payments to Israeli bondholders and transferred $34 million to their own companies. The company owns approximately 30 summer camps and about 80 real estate assets in total, including office and retail properties. The bankruptcy aims to protect these assets and ensure continued operations under a restructuring officer.

data_points.project_notes: Simad Holdings raised $195 million on the Israeli bond market in December, secured by 13 of its camps. The company's bonds fell to junk status and trading was halted after it failed to make an interest payment to bondholders.

articles.title: Paul Massey sells B6 brokerage to Boston-based Horvath & Tremblay
articles.narrative: Paul Massey has sold his brokerage, B6 Real Estate Advisors, to Boston-based Horvath & Tremblay. This acquisition provides a new direction for B6, which had faced financial challenges and broker departures. Horvath & Tremblay, a national brokerage with 17 offices, gains a stronger presence in the New York market and benefits from Massey's extensive industry reputation and experience.

articles.title: Live Local Act megaproject developer sued again – by someone he says he’s never met
articles.narrative: Spanish developer Pablo Castro is facing a third lawsuit concerning his planned HueHub project, a large Live Local Act development in Miami-Dade County. The Miami Gardens Chamber of Commerce alleges that Castro and his Digital Housing Platform entity misappropriated trade secrets, including strategies for securing government worker leases to collateralize financing. Castro denies any knowledge of the chamber or its representatives, calling the lawsuit false and defamatory. This litigation follows two previous lawsuits filed this year against Castro related to the HueHub project, one for unpaid architectural work and another from a former partner alleging she was frozen out of the development after contributing significant work.

data_points.notable_features: Seven 35-story apartment towers with 200,000 square feet of amenities including retail, dining, gardens, a library, and learning center. All apartments are for households earning up to 120 percent of the area median income, exceeding Live Local Act requirements.
data_points.project_notes: Construction is expected to start in July 2026, with financing in the closing process as of May 2026. The project is designed to provide a comfortable lifestyle and feel less like below-market rate housing.

articles.title: Casino mogul picks up $36M mansion next to his Fort Lauderdale home
articles.narrative: Casino mogul James F. Allen and his wife, Shelly Allen, acquired a 38,000-square-foot waterfront mansion adjacent to their current residence in Fort Lauderdale for $35.8 million in an off-market transaction. The property, which previously traded in 2021 for $7.3 million, had an older home demolished and a new three-story, five-bedroom house constructed on the lot. This purchase highlights the strong rebound in Fort Lauderdale's luxury real estate market.

data_points.notable_features: Waterfront property with a new three-story, five-bedroom house, seven bathrooms, five half-bathrooms, a three-car garage, a pool, and a dock.
data_points.project_notes: The property previously sold for $7.3 million in 2021, and an older home on the site was subsequently demolished.

articles.title: MG, Vertical score $100M construction loan for Coral Gables mixed-use
articles.narrative: MG Developer and Vertical Developments secured a $100 million construction loan for Alhambra Parc, a luxury mixed-use condominium project in Coral Gables. The financing, provided by Benmark Capital and arranged by Berkadia, will facilitate the development of 74 condos, 18,000 square feet of retail, and nearly 14,000 square feet of office space. This project marks MG Developer's tenth venture in Coral Gables, where the firm has been a prominent luxury residential developer.

data_points.notable_features: Luxury condo project with rooftop decks, resort-style pool and spa, fitness and wellness facilities, coworking space, and concierge services.
data_points.project_notes: The 1.1-acre site was previously acquired for $19.2 million in 2022. A $14.5 million preconstruction loan was secured in October 2023. Construction is expected to start in Fall 2026.

articles.title: “So much wealth”: Why the rush for waterfront homes rolls on in South Florida
articles.narrative: A waterfront home on North Bay Road in Miami Beach, previously renovated by developer Keith Menin and Rockstar Energy founder Russell Savage, sold for $27 million to a land trust. The property, originally purchased for $16.1 million in April 2024, was on the market for $28 million. This transaction highlights the continued strong demand for luxury waterfront homes in South Florida, driven by a limited inventory of updated properties and an influx of wealth, particularly from California buyers. Brokers note that proposed property tax cuts in Florida are also expected to further stimulate the market.

data_points.address: 5300 North Bay Road, Miami Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront home that was recently renovated.
data_points.project_notes: Property was previously acquired for $16.1 million in April 2024 by Keith Menin.

people.name: Todd Green
people.title: Senior Managing Director and Portfolio Manager

articles.market_intelligence: The Florida Legislature recently voted to send a proposed constitutional amendment to voters in November to raise the homestead exemption on primary residences from $50,000 to $150,000 in 2027 and $250,000 in 2028. Billionaire Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin made significant acquisitions in Coconut Grove and Miami Beach, respectively, for a combined $188 million and $51 million. The proposed wealth tax in California is cited as a factor driving tech buyers to South Florida, with up to 80% of some brokers' deals coming from California.

articles.title: Related Group, Russell Galbut part of Fisher Island team at center of fuel depot battle
articles.narrative: A partnership including Related Group, Russell Galbut, and Raycliff Capital, operating as HRP Group, acquired a nearly 10-acre fuel depot site on Fisher Island for $180 million in October. The developers plan to redevelop the property into "One Fisher Island," but face significant opposition from Miami-Dade County and Fisher Island residents. Miami-Dade County, which previously considered acquiring the site for $400 million, is now pursuing eminent domain to secure continued fuel supply for PortMiami cruise ships, a move the developers are aggressively fighting. Residents also oppose the fuel depot and have filed lawsuits against both the county and HRP Group.

data_points.notable_features: Last remaining development site on Fisher Island, currently a fuel depot servicing PortMiami and cruise ships.
data_points.project_notes: The site's lease is set to expire in 2027. Miami-Dade County is attempting to acquire the site via eminent domain for $400 million, a move opposed by the developers and residents.

articles.title: Bears punt on Illinois, announce move to Indiana
articles.narrative: The Chicago Bears football team has announced its intention to develop a new stadium and entertainment district in Hammond, Indiana, following a prolonged search for a new home. This decision comes after the team's board of directors voted to advance the project in Indiana, citing a failed legislative attempt in Illinois to secure a public-private ownership deal for a stadium in the Chicago suburbs. The proposed Hammond development, which is not yet a finalized deal, is expected to be taxpayer-financed and will include an entertainment area to enhance foot traffic.

data_points.notable_features: Proposed development near Wolf Lake and Gary, approximately 20 miles southeast of Chicago, to include an entertainment area.
data_points.project_notes: The Bears previously acquired the former Arlington International Racecourse property in Illinois for $200 million in 2023, but faced hurdles with property tax relief.

articles.title: Karis Critical’s Hoffman Estates data center proposal dealt setback
articles.narrative: Karis Critical's proposal to rezone the 186-acre Plum Farms property in Hoffman Estates, IL, for a data center development has been dealt a setback after the village's planning commissioners recommended against the rezoning. This marks the second time the data center developer has faced local opposition in the Chicago area, following a similar rejection in Naperville. The decision now moves to the full village board for a final vote, with significant public opposition already voiced.

data_points.project_notes: The 186-acre site was previously acquired by Karis Critical in February 2025 for $45 million from developer Anthony Iatarola. The land was originally envisioned for a mixed-use development with residential, retail, and trails.

articles.title: Kohan Retail completes heavily-discounted purchase of former Citadel Center
articles.narrative: Kohan Retail Investment Group has acquired the former Citadel Center, a 37-story office tower in Chicago, for $137 million. The New York-based firm, known for revitalizing distressed properties, financed the acquisition with $65 million in debt from BayernLB. This purchase marks Kohan's third office acquisition in Chicago within approximately a year, demonstrating their strategy of acquiring discounted assets in the market. The building, which was 45% vacant at the time of the agreement in February, has recently secured new leases, and its naming rights are available for a significant tenant.

data_points.notable_features: 37-story office tower in the Loop district.
data_points.project_notes: The property was previously refinanced for $448 million in 2020. The anchor tenant, Citadel, vacated the property in late 2025.

articles.market_intelligence: Downtown Chicago office vacancy averaged 28% in February.

articles.title: Nuveen snaps up industrial facility in San Antonio
articles.narrative: Nuveen Real Estate has acquired Phase I of Koontz's Westport Industrial Park, a Class A industrial facility on San Antonio's far West Side. The two-building, 187,200-square-foot property, which is 79 percent leased, was valued at nearly $17 million by the Bexar Central Appraisal District, though the sale price was likely higher. Koontz will retain ownership of the adjacent Phase II, which is slated for completion in late 2027.

data_points.address: 438 South Callagahan Road, San Antonio, TX
data_points.notable_features: Class A industrial buildings with direct access to Hwy 151, located in an area of residential expansion.
data_points.project_notes: The Bexar Central Appraisal District valued the buildings at almost $17 million, but the sale price was likely much higher. Koontz will retain ownership of Phase II, an adjacent site set to be completed in Q4 2027. The total project is planned for four buildings.

articles.market_intelligence: Industrial demand in the San Antonio area is holding steady after a sharp increase in the 2020s.

articles.title: Dickies Arena looking to wrangle new naming rights sponsor
articles.narrative: A legal dispute has emerged over the naming rights for Dickies Arena in Fort Worth, TX, as the managing nonprofit, Trail Drive Management, seeks to terminate its agreement with the Dickies brand. The core of the conflict stems from two changes in ownership of the Dickies brand since the 2017 naming rights deal, with Trail Drive alleging that the current owner, Bluestar Alliance, is attempting to avoid contractual obligations. Trail Drive is now actively searching for a new naming rights sponsor for the 560,000-square-foot arena, which opened in 2019.

data_points.notable_features: The arena is home to the Fort Worth Stock Show & Rodeo, concerts, and the Professional Bull Riders World Finals. It is located across from the National Cowgirl Museum & Hall of Fame and within walking distance of the Will Rogers Memorial Center.
data_points.project_notes: The naming rights deal was originally signed in 2017. The Dickies brand headquarters moved from Fort Worth to Costa Mesa, California in 2024.

articles.title: Financing locked in for Preston Center high-rise project
articles.narrative: A joint venture between Willow Bridge Property Company, Ramrock Real Estate, and Lincoln Property Company has secured construction financing for a 12-story mixed-use high-rise at 8300 Douglas Avenue in Dallas. The project, slated for completion in 2028, has already secured Fifth Third Bank as an anchor tenant for its regional headquarters. The financing package, including both equity and construction funds, was provided by an undisclosed lender.

data_points.notable_features: 12-story high-rise in Preston Center.

articles.market_intelligence: Downtown Dallas is experiencing a decline in office occupancy, with companies relocating out of the area. Fifth Third Bank is moving its regional headquarters from Comerica Bank Tower. The Dallas Mavericks and Dallas Stars NBA/NHL franchises are planning to move out of American Airlines Center, and the Neiman Marcus store at 1618 Main Street is closing permanently.

articles.title: Where do the San Antonio Spurs live off the court?
articles.narrative: This article provides an overview of the residential real estate holdings of current and former San Antonio Spurs players and coach, noting a shift from the traditional enclave of The Dominion to more dispersed locations, primarily in north San Antonio near the team's practice facility. It highlights specific property transactions and preferences, such as Keldon Johnson's ranch in Boerne and Gregg Popovich's penthouse in Southtown. The article also touches on the team's upcoming new arena in Downtown San Antonio, which is part of a larger development plan.

articles.title: Jagged little sale: Alanis Morissette quickly unloads Lafayette home for more than $2M above asking
articles.narrative: Singer Alanis Morissette has sold her personal residence at 3958 South Peardale Drive in Lafayette, CA for $9.6 million. The property, listed under Baz Trust, was purchased by Nicholas James Sunseri. The home was on the market for only a week, selling for $2.1 million above its asking price.

data_points.notable_features: The property includes a main house with a wine cellar, library, gym, and a primary suite with a spa-inspired bathroom, outdoor shower, massage room, and yoga space. It also features a separate pool house with a full bathroom and sauna, a swimming pool, outdoor pavilion, exterior kitchen, sports court, and a custom treehouse.

articles.title: Loan defaults pile up at hotels along stretch of San Jose thoroughfare
articles.narrative: The Fontaine Inn, a 52-room hotel in San Jose, has defaulted on a $6.5 million loan from State Bank of Texas. This default is part of a broader trend of distress in the Bay Area's lodging market, with several other hotels in the region, including a nearby 204-room complex and another Super 8 by Wyndham in Livermore, also facing loan defaults or foreclosure. The ongoing challenges highlight the difficult operating environment for hotels in the area.

data_points.project_notes: The property is owned by a group led by Bharat Patel.

articles.market_intelligence: A 204-room hotel complex at 2560 Fontaine Road in San Jose is in default on a $21.7 million loan. A Super 8 by Wyndham in Livermore defaulted on a $7.7 million State Bank of Texas loan in 2022. The 541-room Signia by Hilton hotel in San Jose was taken back by its lender in 2025. The 59-room La Quinta Inn & Suites in San Jose defaulted on a $16 million Central Valley Community Bank loan in May 2026. KSL Capital Partners purchased the Toll House Hotel in Los Gatos through a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure for an unpaid debt of $30 million.

articles.title: 117-year-old family affair over on Union Square
articles.narrative: A historic San Francisco family has sold its majority stake in a 32,400-square-foot retail building in the Union Square district for $19.2 million. The Gump family, who originally purchased the property in 1908, divested their 60.4% interest to a local jeweler, Victor Wu, owner of J. Stella. This transaction marks a significant vote of confidence in the city's retail core, which has faced challenges in recent years, and reflects a return to pre-pandemic investment patterns for trophy assets in the area.

data_points.notable_features: Three-story, renaissance-style palazzo in the heart of the Union Square district.
data_points.project_notes: The Gump family sold their 60.4% stake in the property. The remaining 39.6% stake was granted to local civic endowments in the 1990s and 2010s. The property was originally acquired by the Gump family in 1908.

articles.market_intelligence: Union Square's retail vacancy rate reached 22% post-pandemic. Recent positive developments include pop-ups during Super Bowl week, the return of AT&T, the opening of The RealReal, the acquisition of San Francisco Centre Mall by Presidio Bay Ventures and Prado Group, and a $40 million makeover of Powell Street.

articles.title: Hudson Pacific lists its emptiest SF offices in ongoing property sell-off
articles.narrative: Hudson Pacific Properties has listed its two-building office complex at 875-899 Howard Street in San Francisco's South of Market neighborhood for sale. The 282,828-square-foot property, which the firm acquired in 2007 for $46 million, is being offered for an undisclosed price. The listing is part of Hudson Pacific's strategy to reduce its San Francisco office portfolio amidst internal financial challenges, with the buildings currently representing the emptiest in their local holdings.

data_points.notable_features: The 899 Howard building is 0% occupied and in shell condition, designated for repositioning or conversion. The 875 Howard building is 31.9% occupied and rises six stories.
data_points.project_notes: Hudson Pacific acquired the two buildings in 2007 for $46 million and converted approximately 86,000 square feet of retail and institutional space into offices a decade ago. A pending lease for about 66,000 square feet is in the works for 875 Howard.

articles.title: Texas Family Office Snaps Up Grocery-Anchored El Cajon Retail
articles.narrative: A Texas-based family office has acquired The Shops at Parkway Plaza, a 122,484-square-foot grocery-anchored retail center in El Cajon, for $31.5 million. The property features a strong tenant mix and is strategically located adjacent to a regional shopping mall. The sale highlights the continued robust demand for well-performing daily needs retail centers with potential for net operating income growth.

data_points.notable_features: Grocery-anchored retail center with an investment-grade national tenant roster, located adjacent to Parkway Plaza Mall.

articles.title: Resnick Secures 117K SF of Office, Retail Leases at 250 Hudson St.
articles.narrative: Jack Resnick & Sons has secured 119,000 square feet of new office and retail leases at 250 Hudson St. in New York City, bringing the Hudson Square office tower to 99% occupancy. The transactions include four full-floor office leases and two smaller leases, reflecting the building's broad appeal and Hudson Square's continued evolution as a premier destination for growth industries.

data_points.address: 250 Hudson St., New York City, NY

articles.title: Shallow Bay Industrial Portfolio Trades in East Bay
articles.narrative: Newmark arranged the sale of the Sierra Trinity industrial portfolio, an 11-building shallow bay industrial asset in Dublin, CA. The property was 97% leased to a diverse tenant mix at the time of sale, offering strong in-place cash flow and future upside through rent mark-to-market opportunities. The competitive marketing process for the portfolio highlighted robust demand for multi-tenant, shallow bay industrial product in infill locations.

data_points.notable_features: 11-building shallow bay industrial portfolio.
data_points.project_notes: Average tenant commitment exceeding nine years.

people.name: Steven Golubchik
people.title: Executive Vice Chairman and President, Western Region Capital Markets

people.name: Steven Golubchik
people.title: Executive Vice Chairman and President, Western Region Capital Markets

articles.title: $14M Refinance Secured for Newly Delivered Wicker Park Multifamily
articles.narrative: Arrow Real Estate Advisors has arranged a $14.2 million refinance loan for The Ash, a recently completed 40-unit multifamily property in Chicago's Wicker Park neighborhood. The financing, provided by Associated Bank, was secured while the property was still in its lease-up phase, having already achieved over 70% occupancy. The strong demand for new multifamily developments in this competitive market, coupled with a 30-year tax abatement, contributed to favorable loan terms including a future rate step-down feature upon stabilization.

data_points.project_notes: The property benefits from a 30-year tax abatement through the City’s Affordable Requirements Ordinance program.

articles.title: Marcus & Millichap Brokers Sale of 160-Unit Multifamily Property in Wisconsin
articles.narrative: Marcus & Millichap has brokered the sale of Orchard Courts Apartments, a 160-unit multifamily property in Kenosha, Wisconsin. The property, which includes 20 acres of developable land, attracted significant investor interest due to its stable operations, recent capital improvements, and future development potential. The transaction involved two private investors, with the buyer being procured by Marcus & Millichap.

data_points.notable_features: Includes approximately 20 acres of developable land adjacent to the University of Wisconsin–Parkside.

articles.market_intelligence: The Kenosha market is benefiting from major employment growth driven by companies such as Eli Lilly, Microsoft, and Uline.

articles.title: Brookfield Cuts D.C. Staff, Looks To Sell Part Of The Yards
articles.narrative: Brookfield is significantly reducing its presence in Washington, D.C., with substantial staff layoffs and plans to potentially wind down its local operations. The company is also marketing Parcel Q at The Yards, a 90,000-square-foot waterfront development site, for sale. This move follows a trend of Brookfield offloading D.C.-area office buildings and facing distressed situations within its national office portfolio, reflecting broader market challenges in the region.

data_points.notable_features: Waterfront development opportunity within the 48-acre mixed-use district, approved for residential use with 14,000 SF of retail.
data_points.project_notes: The site was initially planned for office but plans were filed last month to switch to residential, which was approved by the National Capital Planning Commission. The development could reach up to 90 feet tall.

articles.title: TPG Joins Norges, Canadian Pensions On $2B Grocery-Anchored Bet
articles.narrative: A consortium led by TPG Real Estate, alongside PSP Investments, La Caisse, and Norges Bank Investment Management, has acquired Echo Realty, a prominent operator of grocery-anchored retail centers across the Midwest and Southeast. The transaction values Echo Realty at approximately $2 billion. The new ownership group plans to expand Echo's business in both existing and new markets, leveraging its established platforms in leasing and property management. This acquisition underscores a continued institutional investor focus on resilient, needs-based retail segments.

data_points.notable_features: Portfolio includes over 230 retail centers primarily anchored by dominant grocery tenants such as Giant Eagle, Publix, Harris Teeter, Safeway, Acme Markets, and Whole Foods, as well as convenience store anchors.

articles.market_intelligence: Canadian-to-U.S. commercial real estate deal flow decreased by 32% year-over-year to $5 billion in the year ending March, with Canadian firms allocating 37% of international capital to U.S. assets, down from 54% in the prior year.

articles.title: Hotel Performance Surges After Memorial Day Weekend Slump
articles.narrative: U.S. hotels experienced their slowest Memorial Day weekend in six years, with occupancy at 71.1% and demand down 1% year-over-year, marking the lowest rates since 2020. However, performance rebounded sharply in the subsequent days, driven by business travel and major entertainment events. This trend reinforces the K-shaped recovery in hospitality, where luxury and upper-upscale segments continue to grow, while economy hotels face declining demand. Post-holiday, revenue per available room (RevPAR) increased by 8.1% and average daily rates (ADR) by 4.5%, with luxury and upper-upscale leading the recovery.
articles.market_intelligence: The hospitality sector is experiencing a K-shaped economic recovery, with luxury and upper-upscale hotels showing positive growth while branded economy hotels face declining demand. Demand for branded economy hotels has been declining for six consecutive weeks. A 5% decrease in public school students on summer break during Memorial Day weekend compared to the prior year may have contributed to the dip in performance. Despite concerns about rising oil prices and economic uncertainty, travelers are willing to spend on major entertainment events, which boosted hotel performance in several markets.

articles.title: Brookfield Raises $900M For Fund Targeting CRE Liquidity Needs
articles.narrative: Brookfield Asset Management has successfully raised approximately $900 million for its Brookfield Real Estate Solutions II fund, which focuses on general partner-led recapitalizations and equity solutions for real estate assets. The fund, backed by a diverse group of global investors including public pensions and insurance companies, will target value-add returns across North America, the UK, Europe, and Australia. Its primary investment focus will be on the residential and logistics sectors, with potential allocations to self-storage, cold storage, and life sciences properties.

data_points.notable_features: Targets general partner-led recapitalizations and equity solutions for real estate assets.
data_points.project_notes: Predecessor fund, Brookfield Real Estate Solutions Fund I, closed in 2022 with $1.3 billion in commitments.
